title: ttok4bedrock
tagline: Token counting for Amazon Bedrock — a ttok-compatible CLI and SDK

units.title: SDK Public Interface
units.tagline: The two functions users import — count_tokens and truncate
units.description: This module is the public API surface. It exposes two functions that mirror the original ttok library interface, making ttok4bedrock a drop-in replacement. Each function creates a BedrockTokenCounter internally, so SDK users never need to manage the counter lifecycle.
learning_objectives: Understand the public API contract and how it maps to ttok compatibility
See how the SDK delegates to BedrockTokenCounter while hiding complexity
Learn why each call creates a fresh counter instance (and the trade-off involved)

exercises.prompt: Add a third public function get_supported_models() that returns the list of supported model IDs without requiring AWS credentials.
exercises.hint: You can call BedrockTokenCounter.get_supported_models() — it's a classmethod that doesn't need an instance.

units.title: CLI Entry Point
units.tagline: Click-based command-line interface with ttok-compatible flags
units.description: The CLI provides the same flags as ttok (-t, -m, -i) so users can swap ttok for ttok4bedrock without changing scripts. It reads from arguments or stdin, delegates to BedrockTokenCounter, and translates AWS exceptions into user-friendly messages.
learning_objectives: See how Click decorators define a ttok-compatible CLI interface
Understand the input assembly logic (args vs stdin vs file)
Learn the error handling strategy for AWS credential and API failures

exercises.prompt: Add a --verbose flag that prints the number of API calls used during truncation. You'll need to pass return_metadata=True to counter.truncate().
exercises.hint: The metadata dict returned by truncate() includes 'api_calls' and 'final_token_count'.

units.title: Core Token Counting Engine
units.tagline: BedrockTokenCounter — API calls, LRU caching, and adaptive truncation
units.description: This is the heart of the library. BedrockTokenCounter wraps the Bedrock CountTokens API with LRU caching, automatic overhead removal, and an adaptive truncation algorithm that minimizes API calls while finding the exact token boundary.
learning_objectives: Understand how LRU caching wraps the Bedrock API to avoid redundant calls
Learn the overhead removal technique that subtracts message structure tokens
Follow the adaptive truncation algorithm through its three phases: estimation, convergence, and fine-tuning

exercises.prompt: The truncation algorithm uses a fixed limit of 20 API calls. What happens if you lower it to 5? Try with a 10,000-word text truncated to 100 tokens and observe accuracy.
exercises.hint: With fewer iterations the algorithm may not converge to the exact boundary — you'll get a result that's close but not exact.
